title: linanthus ciliatus var. neglectus
intro: Linanthus ciliatus var. neglectus, also known as the Desert Pink Monkey Flower, is a rare and unique plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant are thought to include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and possibly even lowering blood pressure, although further research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or dried extract, and it can also be used in teas and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
